module_mood_contract: Unsettled competence — 'I wonder if this is right?' Don't resolve early; M5 is the rescue.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands per phase + close (8+/10 required; 7 = facilitator-premium flag).
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[0].mood_score=7 (session-open mechanical friction — Teacher Claude covers); phases[1].mood_score=8 (two-folders-filling moment lands); phases[2].mood_score=8; close.mood_score=8. Phase 1 is a 7 (facilitator-premium gap, not a hard fail — Teacher Claude nudge is the fix).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Phase 1 mechanical friction during session open pulls mood to 7. A one-line note naming what the student is watching for ('you're looking for four windows running simultaneously') might lift it to 8 without resolving mood early.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M3 unease must not be resolved by a verification step.
rules_evaluated.evidence: memory/_synthesis-m3.md is the close artifact but it surfaces a contradiction, not a resolution. The synthesizer flags 'where retrievals contradicted what was already in memory' as a load-bearing line. No verification step resolves the unease. Phase 2 and 3 mood notes confirm the open question ('is this memory any good?') carries forward.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — the named teaching moment reliably triggers across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment is 'the feeling: files materialising in two folders at once, four windows churning on one question.' The line in the file: 'The feeling, files materialising in two folders at once, four windows churning on one question, is the lesson.' Trace phase 2 confirms: 'She feels it: this is actually happening.' The moment is structural (files appear in two directories simultaneously — observable by any student who opens the sessions). Not easily skippable: the synthesizer prompt explicitly reads sources/ and writes memory/ as it goes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — the module's Big Idea is honored and the file leads with it (or honors its mood contract).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Agents 101 M3 Big Idea (per content-strategy) is multi-agent shape: separate sessions on shared files — each with its own context, seeing others' work via filesystem. The exercise's 'What happens' and 'The point' sections name exactly this. 'The feeling is the lesson' is the close of the student-facing body. Strategy fidelity: no front-running of M4 (packaged multi-agent) or M5 (rescue / verification).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — the close hands off to the next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: File close: 'Hand off to Three minds, one synthesis (next exercise), which keeps Session 4 open as the main session.' Session 4 (synthesizer) is explicitly named as the continuity thread. Next exercise opens with Session 4 already live — clean mechanical hand-off. Mood at close (unsettled, wondering about memory quality) sets up Three minds' synthesis move without resolving it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work — PRESENCE of conversation cues is REVISE; ABSENCE of pause beats is PASS.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No authored pause cues, no 'discuss with...', no 'share with your neighbor', no 'tell the room' anywhere in the student-facing body. Long agent waits are structural; no authored cue around them. Diagnostic confirmed: absence of conversation cues.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The four forcing moves are all inside fenced prompt blocks: (a) wiki retriever prompt encodes 'ask me to confirm before running anything'; (b) docs retriever prompt encodes 'ask me for three clues'; (c) internet retriever prompt encodes 'ask me to confirm or replace'; (d) synthesizer prompt encodes 'wait for any new content in sources/' and 'write each finding as soon as you have it'. The body prose around the prompts narrates and sets up but does not substitute for the forcing functions. 'Push another round' is an optional prompt given loosely in body — fits the 2026-05-02 carve-out (optional sharpening move, not mandatory teaching beat).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test — does the student leave the chair while the agent works?
rules_evaluated.evidence: The student is instructed to paste prompts and let retrievers run. 'Let them run. Switch between sessions if you want.' The walk-away trust distance is appropriate for M3 — student answers clarifying questions but does not read and re-paste every retrieval line. The synthesizer runs autonomously.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — adults decide. Forced phases, enforced pauses read as paternalistic.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Optional moves ('skip it and proceed with what you have', 'Switch between sessions if you want') are marked as optional. The dead-end-risking move (paste synthesizer prompt) is required. No scripted reaction counts or instinct-suppression prose ('don't scan', 'two rounds max').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Load-bearing platform feature gets no fallback — gate or redesign.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 19: 'Three retrievers is the default; if you can't get one running today (no wiki connector, no doc store reachable, network locked), skip it and proceed with what you have. The lesson works with two retrievers, even one.' The four-session concurrent filesystem fill IS the load-bearing feature of this exercise — as the file itself names: 'Four sessions for one question is more than the work strictly needs... The four is for the feeling.' Offering a one-retriever fallback dilutes exactly what the module exists to teach. Per check_pedagogy.md §42: 'Collapse the parallelism and the seeing parallel writes hit the folder teach is gone, even if the three retrieval files still land.' The fallback prose undercuts the forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Remove or relocate the 'even one retriever works' fallback. Replace with a pre-flight gate note (e.g., 'Before starting: verify you can open four Claude Code sessions in your training directory. If connectors are locked, resolve that with your trainer first') — gate entry, don't dilute the move.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens. Does Claude have access to what it needs?
rules_evaluated.evidence: Retriever prompts correctly ask for connector names inline ('ask which wiki to target if it's ambiguous', 'ask me for three clues'). Claude doesn't guess access. Student brings the specific connector and document clues; Claude uses them. The model of student-brings/Claude-screens is honored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism check — every named file, path, or connector must exist or be constructible.
rules_evaluated.evidence: sources/wiki-retrieval.md, sources/docs-retrieval.md, sources/internet-retrieval.md, memory/_synthesis-m3.md — all written BY the prompts themselves, not assumed pre-existing. crux.md was produced by the prior exercise (name-your-crux). No phantom file references.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All four fenced prompt blocks (wiki retriever, docs retriever, internet retriever, synthesizer) inspected. No [BRACKETS] or placeholder patterns. References are to ./crux.md (existing file) or to sources/ filenames the retriever itself will create. The open-hook pattern is not used — instead, prompts tell Claude to ask for values inline, which is rule 1(a).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Session 1 lead-in: 'In Session 1, tell this session to act as your wiki retriever and stream findings into sources/wiki-retrieval.md.' Session 2: parallel shape. Session 3: parallel shape. Session 4: 'In Session 4, tell this session to act as the synthesizer and integrate the streaming retrievals into memory/.' Push-another-round lead-in: 'Once a retriever returns its first pass... paste this back into that session to push another round.' All under 20 words and command-verb led.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All five prompt blocks follow: lead-in sentence → **Prompt** *(Claude Code)* label → blank line → fenced block. No blockquote wrapping, no *(end of prompt)* closers, no 'copy → ' prefix.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths (Agents 101-specific).
rules_evaluated.evidence: All file writes inside prompts use explicit subpaths: sources/wiki-retrieval.md, sources/docs-retrieval.md, sources/internet-retrieval.md, memory/_synthesis-m3.md. No naked filenames.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named — exercise honors M3 unsettled competence, does not resolve early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Close artifact (memory/_synthesis-m3.md) surfaces a contradiction, not a resolution. Synthesizer prompt: 'That contradiction line is load-bearing — flag it, don't smooth it.' The exercise closes with open questions about memory quality, not with reassurance. Mood contract honored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood — unease is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The student's unease at close is 'is this memory any good?' (epistemic — wondering about quality/correctness) not 'I did something wrong' (punitive). The exercise frame is consistently aspirational: 'Something you do at work alone is being done in parallel in front of you.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The exercise leads with 'Hire three agents to fetch raw material' (positive discipline). The failure mode (fabricated sources, empty retrievals) is addressed inside the prompt blocks as '[NOT FOUND]' instructions — embedded as operational discipline, not as the opening frame.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers — no pre-exercise lecture front-running the pattern.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is an exercise file, not a lecture. No embedded lecture content. The 'why multi-agent' frame is stated minimally in 'The point' (3 sentences) and 'Four sessions for one question' callout — operational framing, not a meta-frame lecture.

stealing_the_mood: Phase 1 (score 7): session-open mechanical friction — student not sure CWD is correct in each new terminal tab. Teacher Claude covers but without the nudge the mood is 7, not 8. Fix is a one-line observational anchor ('you're looking for four windows running simultaneously from the same directory') not an instruction to resolve the uncertainty.
